task_completion.description: Create a Level 1 Data Flow Diagram (DFD) with at least 13 components.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the draw.io canvas (Page 1). Count the shapes representing External Entities, Processes, and Data Stores. There must be at least 13 components. Look for component names matching keywords: browser, mobile, gateway, bank, web, api, auth, processor, fraud, notification, user, transaction, audit.

task_completion.description: Include at least 12 labeled data flows in the DFD.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for directional arrows connecting the components on the DFD. Count the arrows to ensure there are at least 12, and verify that they have text labels describing the data flow.

task_completion.description: Include dashed trust boundaries for Internet, DMZ, Internal Network, and External environments.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for dashed lines or dashed bounding boxes grouping the components. Check for text labels identifying the 4 required zones: 'Internet', 'DMZ', 'Internal Network', and 'External'.

task_completion.description: Create a Threat Catalog on Page 2 with at least 6 STRIDE-categorized threats mapped to DFD elements.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a second page in draw.io (often visible via page tabs at the bottom). The page should contain a list or table of at least 6 specific threats. Verify the threats are categorized using STRIDE (Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, Elevation of Privilege) and that each threat explicitly mentions or maps to a component/flow from the DFD.

task_completion.description: Save the diagram as 'payment_threat_model.drawio' on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the draw.io window title bar, save dialogs, or the Desktop file manager. The file must be named 'payment_threat_model.drawio' and located in '~/Desktop'.

task_completion.description: Export Page 1 as 'payment_threat_model.png' to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for draw.io export dialogs, an image viewer showing the exported DFD, or a file named 'payment_threat_model.png' located on the Desktop.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io graphical interface to build the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: The draw.io application window is visible with toolbars, shape libraries, and the canvas actively being used. The agent did not simply write XML code into a text editor.

integrity.description: The diagram was built progressively rather than instantly generated via a script.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent dragging shapes, typing labels, and connecting arrows over time, rather than the fully completed diagram appearing in a single step.

integrity.description: The PNG file is a genuine export of the draw.io canvas.
integrity.visual_evidence: The exported PNG exactly matches the visual layout of Page 1 in the draw.io application. The agent used the 'File -> Export as -> PNG' menu or similar GUI function.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The DFD must contain exactly or at least 13 components (look for keywords: browser, mobile, gateway, bank, web, api, auth, processor, fraud, notification, user, transaction, audit) and at least 12 labeled data flows. There must be exactly 4 trust boundaries (Internet, DMZ, Internal Network, External). The Threat Catalog on Page 2 must contain at least 6 threats covering the 6 STRIDE categories (Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, Elevation of Privilege). Expected output files are /home/ga/Desktop/payment_threat_model.drawio and /home/ga/Desktop/payment_threat_model.png.
